## [06:03 - 09:06]

So if you have married for ever like I have, I've been married like 26 years now, then you're not necessarily showing up your absolute best every single time you see your spouse. Okay? They haven't been introduced. So this is what I call a blended auto-response sequence. And it's really easy to do. It's like when you go to, all you do is you write these first set of great emails. And then when you go to send a broadcast, like I'm just going to speak in terms of profile or a Weber, what you do is you set up a segment. And that segment would be of your main list. You would say only send the people that are like a message number. So these are the days. Okay, this is day one, day two, day three, day five, day seven. But in a Weber, this would be labeled as message number one. This would be, I know this is counterintuitive, but so that'd be message number four. This would be message number five. This would be message number six. So each message has its own number. And it goes not by the number of days, but by the sequence. So you would say everyone who's an message, above message number six, I want to send that broadcast to. And I know this might sound complicated, it might look complicated, but it's actually very, very simple. You just say, I only want to send this broadcast to people that are beyond message six. And then only these people that you've built that relationship, they're going to be the only ones that get it. So that's the basics on list segmentation. Lots you can do with list segmentation.
